LanguageData.PSR_MaxSkillLevel_Desc: Skills cannot be raised past this rank. Stat scaling is balanced across the whole range: a maxed skill is slightly stronger than vanilla level 20, regardless of what this is set to.
LanguageData.PSR_MaxCharacterLevel_Desc: The pawn level cap. Tracked separately from skills; with variable point costs this leaves leeway and rewards passion investment.
LanguageData.PSR_PointsPerLevel_Desc: Points granted per character level-up. Spent on skill ranks at the passion-based costs below.
LanguageData.PSR_ExpertiseCost_Desc: Point cost per level of a Vanilla Skills Expanded expertise (capped at 20 levels).
LanguageData.PSR_TopEndTempering_Desc: Limits how far outcome chances scale at high skill - surgery success, taming, construct success and similar - so they can't spiral toward 100%. Lower values temper harder. Work speeds and yields scale freely and ignore this, as do curve-bounded stats like melee, shooting and dodge.
LanguageData.PSR_QualityBump_Desc: Once a skill's scaling passes the vanilla-20 equivalent, each bonus vanilla-level adds this chance for one extra crafting quality step.
LanguageData.PSR_XpConversion_Desc: Skill XP earned from work and combat is multiplied by this before feeding the pawn's level. 1 = every point of skill XP counts.
LanguageData.PSR_XpRequirement_Desc: Multiplies the XP needed for each level-up. 1 = the standard curve (100 x level^1.5).
LanguageData.PSR_PassionCostsNote: Points needed to raise a skill by one rank, based on its passion. Passionate skills are cheaper to train. Costs are not refunded if a passion changes later.
LanguageData.PSR_OtherPassions_Desc: Cost for any passion not listed above - modded passions, which usually have variable learning rates.
LanguageData.PSR_CurveNote: Level XP curve: 100 x level^1.5 x requirement multiplier. Skills gain no XP directly and never decay; passions and learning-rate modifiers change how fast the pawn levels instead. Current level cap: {0}
